1.1.name: Greet and introduce oneself or other people
examples: Hello! My name is‚Ä¶ I am a friend of‚Ä¶
Nice to meet you!
This is my colleague.
vocabulary: See you tomorrow /siňź juňź t…ôňąm…ír.…ô ä/

1.2.name: Make small talk
examples: How have you been lately? Busy or not?
The weather is nice today, but it might rain tomorrow.
How was your weekend?
Have you eaten?

1.3.name: Extend, accept, or decline a simple invitation
examples: How about having a meal together at noon?
OK, where shall we go?
Sorry, I have something to do at noon. Next time!

2.1.name: Ask and give basic personal information
examples: What is your surname? What is your name?
I work at Tsinghua University. I am a postdoc.
I don't have children yet.
vocabulary: How old (for children) /ha ä …ô äld f…Ēňź ňąt É…™l.dr…ôn/
How old (general) /ha ä …ô äld ňąd íen.…ôr.…ôl/

2.2.name: Ask and tell one's habits and hobbies
examples: What are your hobbies?
I like playing the piano, playing tennis and reading.
On vacation he likes to travel abroad.

2.3.name: Ask and give information about routine activities
examples: Which day of the week do you work from home?
He goes to work at nine in the morning and gets off at five in the afternoon.
Sometimes he eats at the restaurant downstairs, sometimes he goes out with colleagues.
vocabulary: Go to work /…°…ô ä tuňź w…úňźk/
Go to class /…°…ô ä tuňź kl…Ďňźs/

2.4.name: Ask and give simple directions
examples: Excuse me, where is the nearest cafe?
The bank is not far from here. Go straight, turn left at the second intersection.
vocabulary: How to go /ha ä tuňź …°…ô ä/

2.5.name: Make a simple plan
examples: I want to go to the movies with a friend this weekend.
Next month I will go to New York on a business trip.
This summer I plan to travel to China with my family by plane. We want to go to Beijing first, then Shanghai.

2.6.name: Ask and give a simple description
examples: What does he look like?
My phone is black.
This meeting room is not big, but many people come here for meetings.
vocabulary: Good to drink /…° äd tuňź dr…™Ňčk/

2.7.name: Give a simple narration of an experience
examples: I have been to China, it was two years ago.
Last week I went to Spain for a meeting.

3.1.name: Ask and express likes and dislikes
examples: What do you like to eat?
She really likes this new job.
He doesn't really like living in this place because shopping and transportation are inconvenient.

3.2.name: Ask and respond to preferences
examples: Compared to his previous life, he prefers his current life.
These two computers are different.
This room is brighter. Let's have our meeting here.

3.3.name: Ask and express basic opinions
examples: She is warm and very polite.
I think this letter is too long, what do you think?
For me, writing Chinese characters is the hardest.

4.1.name: Make and cancel a simple appointment or reservation
examples: Shall we meet at ten on Wednesday morning?
I no longer need that large meeting room.

4.2.name: Make and respond to a simple request
examples: May I use this photocopier?
Sure, go ahead.
Ask Mr. Zhang about this question. Here is his phone number.

4.3.name: Make a simple order
examples: I would like a cup of coffee.
How much is this piece of clothing?
It's too expensive, can you make it a little cheaper?
vocabulary: How much /ha ä m Ćt É/

4.4.name: Ask and give simple suggestions/advices
examples: What do you think we should do?
It's too late today. Come back tomorrow.
The meeting is at nine tomorrow. You'd better come a little earlier.

5.1.name: Use linkers to structure discourse and speech
examples: Today let me first introduce two new colleagues, then discuss next month's work, and finally talk about the New Year's party.

I really like living in this place. Why? First, there are shops and supermarkets nearby, so shopping is very convenient. Second, it's not far from my workplace, I can walk to work. Third, there is a park next to it, and I can go jogging there on weekends.

1.name: Basic word order
examples: My surname is Holden.
He gets up at 7 every morning.
Where do you work?
Who are you calling?
They are drinking tea in the office now.
Do you go to work by bus?
I go to the bookstore to buy two books.
He uses a computer to type Chinese characters.
I have dinner with a friend in the evening.

2.name: Word order ‚Äď topicalization
examples: I have already read that document.

3.name: Pronouns
3.notes: Personal: I, you, he, she, you (polite), you (plural), we, they, they (feminine), everyone, oneself; Demonstrative: this, that, other, every, each

5.name: Adverb

6.name: Using śúČ to indicate possession

7.name: Yes-no question with Śźó

8.name: Negative sentences with šłć and ś≤°śúČ

9.name: Elliptical use of ŚĎĘ in a question

14.name: Expressing date and time

16.name: Conjunction

17.name: Indicating age and price
examples: My father is sixty years old.
This book costs ten yuan.
vocabulary: How much /ha ä m Ćt É/

18.name: Use of adjectives and degree adverbs
examples: She is very beautiful.
This book is very interesting.

19.name: Exclamatory sentences
examples: This book is really good!
She is so beautiful!

20.name: Comparative structures
examples: He is still the same as before.
Chinese is different from English.
I have studied Chinese longer than him, but my level is not as high as his.
I like drinking coffee, but I like drinking tea even more.
In our office, he works the longest.

21.name: Indicating quantitative and temporal limits with śúÄ
examples: To do this job, it takes at least a week.
Please let me know by next Monday at the latest.

22.name: Using ŚįĪ to identify a person or object
examples: He is Professor Wang.
That is my home.

23.name: Use ŚįĪ and śČć to indicate earliness and lateness
examples: I arrived at the office at eight in the morning.
I will call you as soon as I get to the office.
She doesn't go to sleep until twelve at night.

24.name: Using Śú® and location particles
examples: Shanghai is in the south of China.
The Red Cross is opposite the United Nations.
His home is near the United Nations.
The cafe is between the bookstore and the supermarket.

25.name: Using particle ÁöĄ to indicate attributives
examples: He gave me a beautiful coat.
This is the tea I bought for you.
The person in front is my sister.

26.name: Using śėĮ or śúČ to indicate the presence/existence
examples: Opposite the bank is a supermarket.
There are many people there.
There are two restaurants on that street.

27.name: Preposition
27.notes: to, for, to, with, from‚Ä¶ to‚Ä¶, towards, toward

28.name: Make a suggestion with particle Śźß
examples: Let's go watch a movie!

29.name: Make a speculation/assumption with the particle Śźß
examples: You are Mr. Wang, aren't you?
This piece of clothing is yours, isn't it?

30.name: Tag questions with śÄéšĻąś†∑, Ś•ĹŚźó, ŚŹĮšĽ•Śźó, Ť°ĆŚźó, śėĮŚźó/śėĮšłćśėĮ
examples: How about going for coffee after work?

31.name: Verb reduplication and verb with šłÄšłč(ŚĄŅ)
examples: Let me think.

32.name: Imperative sentences
examples: Please come in! Please sit!
Don't sit there! Don't speak!

33.name: Modal verbs
examples: I want to buy a cup of coffee.
I have a meeting this afternoon.
I plan to travel to China this summer.
Can you call me now?
You are not allowed to eat here.
He can't speak Chinese.

34.name: Pivotal sentences
examples: I asked him to help me.
I asked her to call me.
My wife wants me to get off work at four today.

35.name: Indirect speech
examples: He said he also works at Tsinghua University.
I asked him where he works.
He asked me if I had a pen.

36.name: Using śėĮ‚Ä¶‚Ä¶ÁöĄ to specify a feature or to emphasize details
examples: My car is black.
That table is round.

37.name: Durative/Progressive aspect
examples: The children are sleeping.
Mr. Wang is in a meeting, please wait a moment.

38.name: Using sentence-final particle šļÜ to inform a new situation or change
examples: It's getting dark.
He is sick.
He no longer works here.
It's seven o'clock, time to go home.
Look, it's going to rain.
You may leave now.

39.name: Using šļÜ to quantify an action or event in the past
examples: I watched a movie on the weekend.
He rested for two hours.
I went to see him three times.

40.name: Using ŤŅá to indicate an experience
examples: I have been to China.

41.name: Using šľö to indicate possibility or likelihood
examples: Do you think the boss will agree?
He should come today.

42.name: Using ÁĚÄ to describe a place or someone's clothing
examples: There is a person standing outside the door.
A painting is hanging on the wall.
He is wearing a black coat.

43.name: Using Śĺó to describe/evaluate an action
examples: He speaks Chinese very quickly.
Your Chinese characters are written very beautifully.

44.name: Simple directional verb complement
examples: When will he come back?
He went out to buy something.

45.name: Using the adverb ŚÜć to express the duplication of an action in the future
examples: If he comes to see me again, tell him I went out for lunch.

46.name: Using the adverb šłÄÁõī to express continuity or indicate straight direction
examples: He has been in meetings from morning until now.
